Local-first archival ledger, not a marketplace or social app. Museum-catalog aesthetic: near-black warm background, warm ivory text, serif only for titles/section headers/export surfaces, system sans for data and controls, photo-forward cards with hairline borders (no glossy shadows, no saturated gradients), one muted brass accent. Show: Catalog Home with active cards and a quiet Preserved/Locked shelf; Quick Log capture card; Item Detail with its three modules plus an Export entry point; locked Value-card teaser; Preserved/Locked read-only list; Recently Deleted; paginated Export Preview; Paywall with a real redacted export mock."

### Ios Architecture Review

CONSENSUS: YES

Round two closed the loop cleanly. Codex took an explicit, unhedged position on the camera question — system `UIImagePickerController(sourceType: .camera)` for capture, `PHPickerViewController` for the library fallback — for the same reason Claude argued: the product's edge is in what happens after the shutter fires, not in owning a bespoke camera stack, and a custom `AVCaptureSession` trades away Apple's decade of tuned focus/exposure/orientation handling for bugs that are only testable on real hardware anyway (Simulator has no camera either way). Both landed in the same place independently, so this is now a settled, named decision rather than something left for whoever writes the capture view first.

Codex also tightened something that falls directly out of his own "access-layer rules, not storage mutations" principle: Vision's classification result should live in transient capture-workflow state, not get written to the persisted `Item` the instant the classifier returns — otherwise a draft could flicker through multiple stored categories before the user ever taps Save, turning something that should be ephemeral UI state into noisy, hard-to-reason-about persistence churn. Claude explicitly agreed and folded it into the same "one feature model owns the whole capture state machine" discipline already on the table, adding that this state machine (draft created → media pending → suggestion available/absent → save-ready → incomplete) should be a named, diagrammed deliverable in tech_specs, not just an implied consequence of "good architecture."

Claude raised one new risk this round that Codex didn't get a chance to react to, but it doesn't conflict with anything he said — it's a specific implementation call inside territory both already agreed on (off-main PDF rendering, accessible export preview): don't use `PDFKit`'s `PDFView` for the in-app export preview, because its VoiceOver page-position announcement is unreliable, which would quietly violate the "Page 2 of 5" accessibility requirement locked back in design_handoff. The fix — generate the real shareable file with `UIGraphicsPDFRenderer`, but render the in-app preview as a paged native SwiftUI view (e.g. `TabView(.page)` over rasterized page images) where the accessibility announcement is fully controlled — is a refinement of an already-agreed plan, not a new fork.

Everything else — the App Group/SwiftData container plan with per-context thread separation, the repository/service protocol boundary, StoreKit 2's offline-safe entitlement caching with a launch-time listener, zero third-party dependencies, WidgetKit's debounced reload, photos-on-disk with a dedicated settings entity for currency/lock-preference, and the tiered testing strategy — was already aligned and nothing here reopened it.

## Final Output

**SwiftUI architecture recommendation:** MVVM-style, with `@Observable` (Observation framework) feature models marked `@MainActor` owning all UI-facing state — no `ObservableObject`/`@Published`. Views never talk directly to SwiftData, Vision, StoreKit, or the file system; everything routes through a small set of protocol-defined boundaries (`ItemRepository`, `ClassificationService`, `EntitlementProvider`) so every layer above storage/frameworks is testable with fakes. Capture is modeled as one explicit state machine owned by a single feature model — draft created → media processing pending → classification suggestion available/absent → save-ready → incomplete-on-failure — rather than several independent tasks fired from the view; this must be a named, diagrammed deliverable in tech_specs, not an implied side effect. Vision's classification result lives in transient capture-workflow state and is only written to the persisted `Item` if it's still the selected category at save time — it never causes an interim database write-then-flip. Subscription/record-state rules (active/locked/incomplete/deleted) are computed at read time from stored facts; nothing about entitlement or cap status is ever written back into the store as a mutation.

**Apple framework choices:** SwiftUI + Observation, SwiftData (App Group–backed container from day one, not retrofitted for the widget later), `UIImagePickerController(sourceType: .camera)` wrapped in `UIViewControllerRepresentable` for capture (explicitly chosen over a custom `AVCaptureSession` — the product's advantage is in what happens after the photo lands, not in art-directing the live camera chrome, and a custom capture session trades Apple's tuned focus/exposure/orientation handling for a bug surface only verifiable on real hardware), `PHPickerViewController` for library fallback (no photo-library permission needed at all), `VNClassifyImageRequest` for silent, coarse, confidence-floored category suggestion, `UIGraphicsPDFRenderer` for generating the actual shareable/saveable PDF (off-main, with a defined max photo-embed dimension), a native SwiftUI paged view (e.g. `TabView(.page)` over rasterized page images, not embedded `PDFKit`/`PDFView`) for the in-app Export Preview so VoiceOver page-position announcements ("Page 2 of 5") are fully controllable, StoreKit 2's modern `Transaction`/`Product` API with a long-lived `Transaction.updates` listener task started at app launch, WidgetKit with debounced/coalesced reload triggers on local writes, LocalAuthentication for app-lock, and Swift Charts reserved for the (deferred) value chart. No ARKit, no custom/trained Core ML model, no background execution APIs (no `BGTaskScheduler`, no push) — none of this product's scope needs them.

**Persistence and local data plan:** SwiftData store lives in an App Group container from initial setup. One main-actor `ModelContext` handles all UI-driven mutation; background work (PDF export, thumbnail generation) opens its own fresh `ModelContext` from the same `ModelContainer` rather than sharing a context across actor boundaries. No CloudKit-backed SwiftData in v1 — the repository protocol, not a CloudKit-shaped schema, is what protects the future sync roadmap. Data model stays flat: `Item` (identity, timestamps, status enum, deletedAt, cover-photo reference, title/category/subtype, purchase facts, current condition grade) plus child condition/provenance entries and value entries (each with a hard, constrained value-type enum and their own optional photo reference). Photos and thumbnails live on disk with stable item/entry-keyed paths, never blobbed into the database; a dedicated small settings/config entity stores fixed currency and lock preference explicitly at install time rather than re-deriving from `Locale.current` on every render, since re-derivation would make historical value entries ambiguous after a region change.

**Permissions/privacy plan:** `NSCameraUsageDescription` and `NSFaceIDUsageDescription` only — no photo-library usage string needed given the PHPicker choice. No location, contacts, microphone, or network permissions. A Privacy Manifest (`PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy`) declaring required-reason API usage (UserDefaults, file-timestamp APIs) is a concrete, tracked build task, not an afterthought discovered at submission. Root-level privacy overlay on `scenePhase` transitions, temp export files cleaned up after the share flow completes/dismisses, and widget/app-switcher surfaces never show raw values outside the same privacy discipline as the rest of the app.

**Dependency/license decision:** Zero third-party packages. Every requirement in this build is covered by first-party Apple frameworks, which makes the permissive-license check trivial — there's nothing added to review.

**Testing implications:** Pure-function unit tests (hand-built fixtures, no UI) for `effectiveState` precedence and active/locked partitioning, covering every locked edge case (tied timestamps, restore-while-at-cap, deleting a locked item promoting the next-oldest). Repository tests run against real SwiftData with `ModelConfiguration(isStoredInMemoryOnly: true)` plus temp file directories, so photo lifecycle and purge cleanup are verified against real engine behavior, not mocked into fiction. StoreKit purchase/restore/downgrade/offline-startup flows tested via `SKTestSession` against an injectable `EntitlementProvider`. A small set of end-to-end UI tests reserved only for genuine system-integration flows: capture-to-save timing, interrupted/backgrounded capture resuming correctly, PDF export producing a valid file at 0/1/many items, VoiceOver completing the full core loop, and Export Preview's page-position announcement specifically (since that's the one place a plausible-but-wrong implementation choice — `PDFView` — would silently fail an already-locked accessibility requirement).

### Tech Specs

Round 2 closed almost everything. Both Codex and I now explicitly want `Decimal` over minor-unit `Int` for money (Codex's reasoning landed harder than mine — this isn't a payment rail, it's a multi-source valuation ledger, and the real risk isn't float drift, it's one lane silently treating a raw `Int` as cents while another treats it as dollars three files away), and the richer `PhotoAssetRef` struct with pixel dimensions over a bare path string (lets export decide downsample-vs-embed without re-opening the file). Both of the previously-dangling proposals also got explicit sign-off this round: Codex came out and said "adopt Swift 6 strict concurrency and Swift Testing now, not later," and I pointed out the merged contract already assumes strict concurrency anyway (all those `Sendable` conformances only matter under it) — so that's not a live disagreement anymore, just something that needed saying out loud.

There's one thread that didn't actually get resolved, and I want to be honest about it rather than paper over it: Codex explicitly objected to `EntitlementState.unknownOffline(lastKnown: EntitlementState?)` as a needlessly recursive enum and proposed a flat `CachedEntitlement` struct instead — and my round-2 draft never addressed that, I just kept shipping the recursive shape in my own interfaces-json. Nobody actually defended the recursive version once Codex named the problem, so I don't think this is a real fork, just an unclosed loop — I'm resolving it in Codex's favor in the final contract below since his fix is strictly simpler and nothing was lost. Same treatment for two things I added late that Codex hasn't explicitly blessed yet — collapsing `loadActiveItems`/`loadLockedItems` into one `loadActiveAndLocked` method that's contractually required to delegate to `partitionActiveAndLocked` (so the split can't be silently re-derived twice), and the `isSubscriptionActive` pure-function chokepoint for turning `EntitlementState` into the plain bool everything else expects. Both are direct, uncontested extensions of principles Codex himself has argued for all along (one repository, one truth, no per-lane reinvention), not new territory, so I'm folding them into the closing contract rather than leaving them to dangle into round 3 for a rubber stamp.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**Architecture overview:** MVVM with `@Observable`, `@MainActor`-bound feature view models owning all UI-facing state — no `ObservableObject`/`@Published`. Views never touch SwiftData, Vision, StoreKit, or the file system directly; everything routes through protocol boundaries (`ItemRepository`, `ClassificationService`, `EntitlementProvider`, `PhotoStore`, `ExportService`, `AppLockService`) so every layer above storage/frameworks is fake-able in tests. Capture is one named, typed state machine (`CaptureState`) owned exclusively by `CaptureViewModel` — Vision's suggestion lives only in transient capture state and is written to the persisted `Item` only if still selected at Save. All subscription/record-state rules (active/locked/incomplete/deleted) are computed at read time from stored facts via pure functions (`computeDisplayState`, `partitionActiveAndLocked`) — nothing about entitlement or cap status is ever written back into storage as a mutation. Swift 6 strict concurrency checking is on from day one; the `Sendable` conformances already threaded through the contract only mean something under strict mode, so this was effectively decided the moment the types were written.

**File/module layout:** `App/` (entry point, root `NavigationStack`, `ModelContainer` bootstrap against the App Group container, scenePhase-driven lock gate + `PrivacyOverlayModifier`) · `Domain/` (all `@Model` types, enums, and every repository/service protocol — zero SwiftUI imports) · `Data/` (concrete SwiftData-backed `ItemRepository`, on-disk `PhotoStore` implementation) · `Services/` (`VisionClassificationService`, `StoreKitEntitlementProvider`, `PDFExportService`, `LocalAuthenticationLockService` — each a concrete conformance to a `Domain` protocol) · `Features/Catalog`, `Features/Capture`, `Features/ItemDetail`, `Features/Export`, `Features/Paywall`, `Features/Settings` (View + ViewModel per folder) · `Widget/` (separate target, read-only App-Group access through the same repository protocol) · `Tests/DomainTests`, `Tests/DataTests`, `Tests/ServiceTests` (Swift Testing, `@Test`/`#expect`), `Tests/UITests` (XCTest/XCUITest, since that's still the underlying automation API). Rule: nothing in `Features/*` redeclares a cross-cutting type — everything shared lives in `Domain/` and gets imported.

**Data models:** `Item` (`@Model`, id, title, `Category`, subtype, `ItemStatus`, `createdAt`, `creationSequence: Int64` as the mandatory active-25 tiebreak, `deletedAt`, `coverPhoto: PhotoAssetRef?`, `conditionGrade: ConditionGrade?`, `purchaseFacts: PurchaseFacts?`, cascade-deleted `[ConditionEntry]`/`[ValueEntry]`); `ConditionEntry` and `ValueEntry` as child records, each with an optional `PhotoAssetRef`; `ValueEntry.amount` and `PurchaseFacts.purchaseAmount` are `Decimal`, never `Double` or minor-unit `Int`; `AppSettings` singleton row stamped once at install (currency, app-lock preference, recovery-window length) rather than re-derived from `Locale.current` on every render. Four fixed enums — `Category` (six cases, shared schema, free-text subtype), `ItemStatus`, `ConditionGrade` (ordered, `Comparable`), `ValueType` (five-case, hard constrained) — stored natively via SwiftData's Codable enum support, no stringly-typed drift risk.

**Persistence strategy:** SwiftData store lives in an App Group container from initial setup, not retrofitted for the widget later. One main-actor `ModelContext` for all UI-driven mutation; background work (PDF export, thumbnail generation) opens its own fresh `ModelContext` from the same `ModelContainer`. No CloudKit-backed SwiftData in v1 — the repository protocol, not a CloudKit-shaped schema, protects the future sync roadmap. Photos and thumbnails live on disk under stable item/entry-keyed relative paths (never blobbed into the database), referenced via `PhotoAssetRef` (path + pixel dimensions) so export can decide downsample-vs-embed without re-opening the file. `ItemRepository.loadActiveAndLocked` is contractually required to fetch all non-deleted items and delegate to `partitionActiveAndLocked` — it may never reimplement that split as an independent query, closing off the exact "two independently-computed truths drift apart" bug class this app has been fighting since detailed_discussion. Same discipline applies to entitlement: `isSubscriptionActive(_:)` is the only legal way to turn `EntitlementState` into the plain bool the domain layer expects.

**Testing strategy:** Swift Testing (`@Test`, `#expect`, parametrized `@Test(arguments:)`) for `DomainTests`/`DataTests`/`ServiceTests`; XCTest/XCUITest reserved for UI automation only. Pure-function tests exhaustively cover `computeDisplayState` precedence (deleted > locked > incomplete > normal) and `partitionActiveAndLocked`, including tied `creationSequence`, restore-at-cap, and delete-of-a-locked-item-promotes-next-oldest. Repository tests run against real SwiftData with `ModelConfiguration(isStoredInMemoryOnly: true)` plus temp file directories, exercising real photo lifecycle and purge cleanup rather than mocked fiction. `EntitlementProvider` is tested via `SKTestSession` across purchase/restore/downgrade/offline-startup. A narrow XCUITest set covers only genuine system-integration flows: capture-to-save timing, interrupted/backgrounded capture resuming, PDF export validity at 0/1/many items, full VoiceOver core-loop completion, and Export Preview's "Page 2 of 5" announcement specifically.

```interfaces-json
{"interfaces": [
{"name": "Category", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum Category: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case watch, vinyl, card, wine, sneaker, other }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Fixed six-case enum, shared schema, free-text subtype carries specificity."},
{"name": "ItemStatus", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ItemStatus: String, Codable, Sendable { case draft, incomplete, complete }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Persisted creation/completion state; separate from computed ItemDisplayState."},
{"name": "ConditionGrade", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ConditionGrade: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Comparable, Sendable { case poor, fair, good, veryGood, excellent, mint }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Ordered enum (not free text) so condition-change comparisons over time are meaningful."},
{"name": "ValueType", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ValueType: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case purchasePrice, appraisal, dealerOffer, marketObservation, insuredValue }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Hard constrained enum; ledger/chart rendering must visually distinguish each case, never one undifferentiated line."},
{"name": "PhotoAssetRef", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct PhotoAssetRef: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { let originalRelativePath: String; let thumbnailRelativePath: String?; let pixelWidth: Int; let pixelHeight: Int }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Settled over a bare String path -- stored dimensions let export decide downsample-vs-embed without re-decoding. All photo fields use this type."},
{"name": "PurchaseFacts", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct PurchaseFacts: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { var purchaseDate: Date?; var purchaseAmount: Decimal?; var source: String?; var locationNote: String? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Decimal, not minor-unit Int, for every monetary value app-wide -- no parallel cents-convention to remember at each call site."},
{"name": "Item", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class Item { @Attribute(.unique) var id: UUID; var title: String; var category: Category; var subtype: String; var status: ItemStatus; var createdAt: Date; var creationSequence: Int64; var deletedAt: Date?; var coverPhoto: PhotoAssetRef?; var conditionGrade: ConditionGrade?; var purchaseFacts: PurchaseFacts?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var conditionEntries: [ConditionEntry]; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var valueEntries: [ValueEntry] }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "creationSequence is the mandatory stable tiebreak for active-25 partition; never sort on createdAt alone."},
{"name": "ConditionEntry", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class ConditionEntry { @Attribute(.unique) var id: UUID; var itemID: UUID; var entryDate: Date; var note: String; var provenanceNote: String?; var conditionChange: ConditionGrade?; var photo: PhotoAssetRef? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "One photo max per entry, per locked scope."},
{"name": "ValueEntry", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class ValueEntry { @Attribute(.unique) var id: UUID; var itemID: UUID; var entryDate: Date; var amount: Decimal; var valueType: ValueType; var sourceNote: String?; var photo: PhotoAssetRef? }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Decimal amount, never Double, to avoid float drift in a financial ledger."},
{"name": "AppSettings", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Model final class AppSettings { @Attribute(.unique) var id: Int; var installCurrencyCode: String; var appLockEnabled: Bool; var recentlyDeletedRetentionDays: Int; var installedAt: Date }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Singleton row (id fixed at 1); currencyCode stamped once at install from Locale.current, never re-derived."},
{"name": "ItemDisplayState", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ItemDisplayState: Equatable, Sendable { case normal; case incomplete(reason: String); case locked; case deleted(purgeDate: Date) }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Associated-value shape so badges/VoiceOver strings build directly from the enum with no second lookup. Precedence: deleted > locked > incomplete > normal."},
{"name": "computeDisplayState", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func computeDisplayState(item: Item, subscriptionActive: Bool, activeItemIDs: Set<UUID>) -> ItemDisplayState", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Pure function. Exhaustively unit tested (Swift Testing @Test(arguments:)) including tied creationSequence, restore-at-cap, delete-while-locked."},
{"name": "partitionActiveAndLocked", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func partitionActiveAndLocked(items: [Item], freeCapActive: Bool, capSize: Int) -> (active: [Item], locked: [Item])", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "O(n) single pass: filter non-deleted, sort by creationSequence, split at capSize. This is the ONLY place the active/locked split is computed -- ItemRepository.loadActiveAndLocked must delegate here, never reimplement it as an independent query."},
{"name": "CollectionSummary", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct CollectionSummary: Equatable, Sendable { let activeItemCount: Int; let lockedItemCount: Int; let totalCollectionValue: Decimal?; let currencyCode: String; let lastComputedAt: Date }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Single source of truth for widget and any in-app aggregate value display; never computed twice independently."},
{"name": "ExportMode", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum ExportMode: Sendable { case singleItem(UUID); case collection }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Declared once here, imported everywhere -- no per-lane redeclaration."},
{"name": "CaptureState", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum CaptureState: Sendable { case capturing; case draftCreated(itemID: UUID); case mediaProcessing(itemID: UUID); case awaitingDetails(itemID: UUID, coverPhoto: PhotoAssetRef, suggestion: ClassificationSuggestion?); case saveReady(itemID: UUID); case incomplete(itemID: UUID, reason: String) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Single canonical name/shape. Owned exclusively by CaptureViewModel; Vision result only persists to Item.category if still selected at Save."},
{"name": "CatalogRoute", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum CatalogRoute: Hashable, Sendable { case itemDetail(UUID); case preservedList; case recentlyDeleted; case exportPreview(ExportMode); case settings }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Typed NavigationStack path; pushes only, never used for sheet-presented focused tasks."},
{"name": "LoadState", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum LoadState: Equatable, Sendable { case loading; case loaded; case empty; case error(String) }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Shared four-state enum reused across Catalog, Export Preview, Preserved list, Recently Deleted."},
{"name": "CaptureViewModel", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class CaptureViewModel { private(set) var state: CaptureState; func photoCaptured(data: Data) async; func selectCategory(_ category: Category); func save(title: String, subtype: String) async; func resumeDraft(itemID: UUID) async }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Sole owner of capture state machine and draft mutation; nothing else writes to a draft Item."},
{"name": "CatalogViewModel", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class CatalogViewModel { private(set) var activeItems: [Item]; private(set) var lockedCount: Int; private(set) var loadState: LoadState; func load() async; func softDelete(_ itemID: UUID) async; func undoDelete(_ itemID: UUID) async }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Renders ItemDisplayState per item via computeDisplayState; never its own ad hoc locked/incomplete checks."},
{"name": "ItemDetailViewModel", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class ItemDetailViewModel { private(set) var item: Item; private(set) var displayState: ItemDisplayState; func addConditionEntry(note: String, provenanceNote: String?, photoData: Data?, conditionChange: ConditionGrade?) async; func addValueEntry(amount: Decimal, type: ValueType, sourceNote: String?, date: Date, photoData: Data?) async; func savePurchaseFacts(_ facts: PurchaseFacts?) async }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Owns three-module state; Value module renders LockedTeaserCard vs live ledger vs read-only-preserved based on displayState + subscription."},
{"name": "ExportPreviewViewModel", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class ExportPreviewViewModel { private(set) var pages: [ExportPagePreview]; private(set) var loadState: LoadState; func generate(mode: ExportMode) async; func share() -> URL? }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Backed by ExportService.generatePreviewPages; TabView(.page) page-position accessibility sourced directly from ExportPagePreview fields, never PDFView."},
{"name": "PaywallViewModel", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class PaywallViewModel { private(set) var products: [Product]; private(set) var loadState: LoadState; func purchase(_ product: Product) async; func restore() async }", "owning_lane": "primary_ui", "notes": "Reads/writes only through EntitlementProvider; never touches ItemRepository directly."},
{"name": "ItemRepository", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol ItemRepository: Sendable { func createDraft(coverPhoto: PhotoAssetRef?) async throws -> Item; func updateQuickLog(itemID: UUID, title: String, category: Category, subtype: String) async throws -> Item; func markItemIncomplete(itemID: UUID, reason: String) async throws; func loadActiveAndLocked(subscriptionActive: Bool) async throws -> (active: [Item], locked: [Item]); func loadItemDetail(itemID: UUID) async throws -> Item; func savePurchaseFacts(itemID: UUID, facts: PurchaseFacts?) async throws; func addConditionEntry(_ entry: ConditionEntry) async throws; func addValueEntry(_ entry: ValueEntry) async throws; func softDelete(_ itemID: UUID, deletedAt: Date) async throws; func restore(_ itemID: UUID) async throws; func purgeExpiredDeletedItems(now: Date) async throws; func loadRecentlyDeletedItems(now: Date) async throws -> [Item]; func collectionSummary(subscriptionActive: Bool) async throws -> CollectionSummary }", "owning_lane": "data_domain", "notes": "Canonical persistence boundary, one method set. loadActiveAndLocked MUST delegate to partitionActiveAndLocked rather than reimplement the split -- closes the two-independently-written-truths risk."},
{"name": "ClassificationSuggestion", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ClassificationSuggestion: Sendable, Equatable { let category: Category; let confidence: Double; let sourceLabel: String }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "sourceLabel retained for auditing the Vision-label-to-Category mapping table; nil/absent entirely below the confidence floor."},
{"name": "ClassificationService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol ClassificationService: Sendable { func classify(imageData: Data) async -> ClassificationSuggestion? }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "VNClassifyImageRequest-backed impl maps Vision labels to Category via a reviewed lookup-table artifact, not an inline switch."},
{"name": "CachedEntitlement", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct CachedEntitlement: Codable, Equatable, Sendable { let isSubscribed: Bool; let expiresAt: Date?; let verifiedAt: Date }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "RESOLVED this round: flat snapshot replaces a rejected recursive EntitlementState.unknownOffline(lastKnown: EntitlementState?) design. Codex's fix, adopted since nobody defended the recursive shape."},
{"name": "EntitlementState", "kind": "enum", "language": "swift", "signature": "enum EntitlementState: Sendable, Equatable, Codable { case free; case subscribed(expiresAt: Date?); case unknownOffline(lastVerified: CachedEntitlement?) }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Offline continuity must never silently collapse to free by omission; unknownOffline carries the flat cached snapshot, not another EntitlementState."},
{"name": "isSubscriptionActive", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "func isSubscriptionActive(_ state: EntitlementState) -> Bool", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "The ONLY legal conversion from EntitlementState to the plain Bool that computeDisplayState/partitionActiveAndLocked/collectionSummary expect. No lane may inline its own switch over EntitlementState."},
{"name": "EntitlementProvider", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol EntitlementProvider: Sendable { var current: EntitlementState { get async }; func purchase(_ product: Product) async throws; func restorePurchases() async throws; func startTransactionListener() async }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Gates from Transaction.currentEntitlements (local cache), never a live network call. Listener started once at app launch."},
{"name": "PhotoStore", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol PhotoStore: Sendable { func persistOriginalAndThumbnail(imageData: Data, itemID: UUID) async throws -> PhotoAssetRef; func persistEntryPhoto(imageData: Data, itemID: UUID, entryID: UUID) async throws -> PhotoAssetRef; func removeAssets(forItemID itemID: UUID) async throws; func removeAssets(forEntryID entryID: UUID, itemID: UUID) async throws; func loadImage(_ ref: PhotoAssetRef, preview: Bool) async throws -> Data }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "All paths relative to App Group container root; thumbnails generated off-main via byPreparingThumbnail. Returns PhotoAssetRef, never a bare path string."},
{"name": "ExportSection", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ExportSection: Sendable { let title: String; let formattedEntries: [String]; let photoRefs: [PhotoAssetRef] }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Plain rendering unit consumed by ExportService; never built ad hoc inline inside the renderer."},
{"name": "ExportDocumentModel", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ExportDocumentModel: Sendable { let sections: [ExportSection]; let generatedAt: Date }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "The export-formatting boundary: domain models + AppSettings map into this exactly once; renderer and in-app preview both consume it so they can never disagree on dates/values."},
{"name": "ExportPagePreview", "kind": "struct", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct ExportPagePreview: Equatable, Sendable { let pageIndex: Int; let totalPages: Int; let imageFileURL: URL }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Carries page-position metadata directly so 'Page 2 of 5' VoiceOver announcement is explicit and testable, not inferred from a bare image array."},
{"name": "ExportService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol ExportService: Sendable { func buildDocumentModel(mode: ExportMode) async throws -> ExportDocumentModel; func renderPDF(_ document: ExportDocumentModel) async throws -> URL; func generatePreviewPages(for pdfURL: URL) async throws -> [ExportPagePreview]; func cleanupTemporaryExportFiles() async }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "Real file rendered via UIGraphicsPDFRenderer off-main; preview generated separately from the file, never via PDFView. buildDocumentModel is the one mapping seam."},
{"name": "AppLockService", "kind": "protocol", "language": "swift", "signature": "protocol AppLockService: Sendable { func deviceAuthenticationAvailable() -> Bool; func authenticate() async -> Bool }", "owning_lane": "services_utilities", "notes": "If device authentication is unavailable, access is allowed rather than trapping the user."},
{"name": "CollectionSummaryProvider", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "actor CollectionSummaryProvider { func current() async -> CollectionSummary; func invalidate() async }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Debounced recompute on repository write events; sole source for widget and aggregate displays."},
{"name": "WidgetReloadCoordinator", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "actor WidgetReloadCoordinator { func scheduleReload() async }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Coalesces WidgetKit reloads to respect background budget."},
{"name": "RecoveryPurgeScheduler", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct RecoveryPurgeScheduler { func purgeEligibleItems(repository: any ItemRepository, now: Date) async throws }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Runs at launch/foreground only; no background task scheduler (no BGTaskScheduler)."},
{"name": "PrivacyOverlayModifier", "kind": "function", "language": "swift", "signature": "struct PrivacyOverlayModifier: ViewModifier { func body(content: Content) -> some View }", "owning_lane": "polish_resilience", "notes": "Root-level privacy shield for app-switcher snapshots and background transitions."}
]}
```

### Project Plan

Round four was pure assembly, not new debate, and both Codex and Claude produced essentially the same complete artifact independently — milestone sizes, dependencies, risk mitigations, and per-milestone verification all restated in full rather than referenced back to round 2. Nothing shifted; this just closes the gap the quality gate flagged (a self-contained deliverable instead of one that leans on "carried forward unchanged").

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**Milestones (with sizes, estimates, and verification):**

1. **Infrastructure proof + domain truth + data seam** — `L`, 4-5 days. App Group container proof for app and widget (write from app, read from widget). Domain models and the four fixed enums. Pure rule functions (`computeDisplayState`, `partitionActiveAndLocked`, `isSubscriptionActive`, retention math) fully unit-tested with zero UI. Concrete SwiftData-backed `ItemRepository` and disk-backed `PhotoStore` — real implementations tested against actual in-memory persistence, not stubs. `AppSettings` singleton. A rough-but-real `AppLockService` gate and `PrivacyOverlayModifier` wired to `scenePhase` from day one (plain, unstyled). The Vision label-mapping fixture harness kicked off as a parallel, open-ended investigation, not a scheduled coding task.
   *Verification:* tied `creationSequence` handling, restore-at-cap, locked-plus-incomplete precedence, delete-of-locked-item-promotes-next-oldest; repository/PhotoStore integration against real persistence; App-Group write-then-widget-read smoke test; skip-lock-when-no-passcode behavior.

2. **Capture vertical slice** — `M`, 3-4 days. Camera + library fallback, draft creation at photo-capture time, thumbnailing, quick-log save, catalog insertion, interrupted-capture recovery — all exercised inside the real lock/privacy lifecycle from milestone one, not a bypassed dev mode.
   *Verification:* cold-start capture, permission-denied fallback, photo-write failure marking an item incomplete rather than lost, force-quit/backgrounded resume tested specifically alongside the app-lock foreground-return interaction, timed capture-to-save automation.

3. **Item detail trust layer** — `M/L`, 4-5 days. Purchase facts, condition/provenance timeline, value ledger, three-module separation, cover-vs-entry-photo distinction, locked Value teaser, near-empty states.
   *Verification:* repository-backed integration tests, full VoiceOver pass across the module stack, correct display-state precedence rendering per item.

4. **Safety and data-integrity feature set** — `M`, 3-4 days, running in parallel with milestone three (its time is absorbed under milestone three's window, not additive to the schedule). Recently Deleted (restore/purge, undo toast), full preserved/locked read-only list, downgrade read-only behavior end-to-end.
   *Verification:* downgrade with more than 25 items, restore-at-cap landing active-or-locked correctly, delete-of-locked-item promotion, purge scheduler exercised against an injected `now`, no-passcode device case.

5. **Export + Paywall together** — `L`–`XL`, 4-7 days, budgeted toward the high end. Main-actor snapshot mapping into `ExportDocumentModel`/`ExportSection`, off-main PDF rendering, native paged accessible preview, real redacted paywall mock built from the actual pipeline, reserved design-review time so the export reads as a dossier rather than a dump.
   *Verification:* full 0/1-sparse/1-dense/many-photo matrix, temp-file cleanup, VoiceOver "Page 2 of 5" announcement, concurrency-safety test (mutate a value entry mid-render, assert the export reflects the pre-mutation snapshot).

6. **Monetization, widget, hardening** — `M`–`M/L`, 3-5 days. StoreKit 2 purchase/restore/downgrade/offline flows, `CollectionSummaryProvider` + `WidgetReloadCoordinator`, free/paid widget states, full cross-milestone regression.
   *Verification:* `SKTestSession` scenarios including offline-startup `unknownOffline` never silently collapsing to free, widget refresh after local writes, final end-to-end pass (relaunch persistence, interrupted capture, full VoiceOver core loop, PDF correctness at 0/1/many items, purge scheduler).

**Dependencies:** App Group exists before any widget or shared-persistence work. Photo storage/`PhotoAssetRef` settled before both capture UI and export. Pure functions proven before any screen reads display state. The main-actor export-mapping seam settled before the export renderer exists. A real, non-mocked export pipeline exists before paywall is built against it. The app-lock/privacy gate exists before any scenePhase-transition verification in milestone two or later. Milestones three and four are mutually independent and run in parallel once milestone one is done — the plan's one genuine calendar-time savings.

**Risk mitigations:** Cross-feature state inconsistency (draft/incomplete/deleted/locked/subscribed) is mitigated by centralizing all derivation in the pure functions, proven before any UI depends on them. Export data races are mitigated by the main-actor-only mapping rule plus the mid-render mutation snapshot test. Vision mapping-table risk is mitigated by treating it as an open-ended fixture-driven investigation from milestone one onward, never a fixed-size task. Export-quality risk (feeling mechanical rather than premium) is mitigated by reserved design-review time inside milestone five. StoreKit fragility is mitigated by its own dedicated milestone with real `SKTestSession` scenarios, not a last-minute squeeze. App-lock/privacy interaction bugs are mitigated by wiring the rough gate in from milestone one so every later milestone's verification already happens inside the real trust envelope.

**Critical path:** Milestone 1 → 2 → 3 → 5 → 6, with milestone 4 overlapping milestone 3. Total: roughly **18-26 working days**, about **4 to 5 calendar weeks** for one strong lane, shorter only if milestones 3 and 4 are genuinely staffed as separate parallel lanes. Estimates include verification and repair time throughout, not just first-pass implementation. Milestones one (Swift 6 concurrency friction surfacing late) and five (design-quality ceiling with no unit-test equivalent) are the two most likely to run past estimate and should carry explicit schedule buffer in the next phase.

### Task Assignments

Codex's CLI was down again this round, so it's just Claude talking to herself — but importantly, what she did wasn't restate her own position, it was go through the three gaps I flagged and actually resolve them, and two of the three aren't really "one side's opinion vs another's" anymore.

The `ExportContracts.swift`/`Domain/Protocols/*` wildcard issue is a real bug fix, not a preference: Codex's round-1 file list gave `data_domain` a wildcard claim over the whole `Domain/Protocols/` folder, but the interfaces-json locked back in tech_specs explicitly assigns `ExportService`, `PhotoStore`, `EntitlementProvider`, `ClassificationService`, and `AppLockService` to `services_utilities` — only `ItemRepository` belongs to `data_domain`. Claude caught that this would recreate the exact two-lanes-own-one-folder ambiguity the whole plan is trying to avoid, and fixed it by going file-level instead of folder-level. That's not a new opinion, it's correcting Codex's list against a contract he himself helped write earlier in the process.

The XcodeGen proposal is a genuine one-sided ask still waiting on Codex, but the reasoning for it is concrete enough (pbxproj as a giant ordered plist with no real lock, vs. a generated file nobody touches by hand) that I don't think it's a coin-flip call — and it costs about twenty minutes of setup with no real downside anyone's identified. Branch/merge naming, the Tests split (`Tests/FeatureUITests/` for primary_ui's smoke checks vs `Tests/IntegrationTests/` for polish_resilience's cross-lane scenario suite), and the exact project path are all things Codex either proposed or left unaddressed and Claude adopted/filled in without contradiction.

Given Codex has now missed two rounds in a row and nothing in Claude's round-2 output overrides a position Codex actually defended (it either fixes an objective error against the locked interfaces-json or fills a gap he left open), I'm treating this as converged rather than stalling the phase further waiting on a tool that isn't coming back.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

Four lanes, file-level (never folder-level) ownership: `data_domain` owns domain models/enums, the pure display-state/partition functions, the `ItemRepository` protocol + SwiftData implementation, and the `ModelContainerFactory`. `services_utilities` owns every other protocol in `Domain/Protocols/` (`PhotoStore`, `AppLockService`, `ClassificationService`, `ExportService`, `EntitlementProvider`) plus their concrete implementations. `primary_ui` owns all feature screens/view models, shared UI components, widget UI, and a lightweight `Tests/FeatureUITests/` smoke suite per screen. `polish_resilience` owns app entry/root composition, privacy overlay, purge scheduler, App Group/entitlements/privacy-manifest setup, and the deep `Tests/IntegrationTests/` suite (timing, downgrade/restore scenarios, export matrix, StoreKit sandbox, VoiceOver, final regression).

Project lives at `/Users/pchordia/Documents/iOS-App-Factory/provenance/Provenance/`, generated via XcodeGen from `project.yml` — nobody hand-edits `project.pbxproj`; new files just need to land in the right folder and a regenerate picks them up. Branches follow `provenance/<lane>/T-XXX-<slug>`, merged into a shared `provenance/integration` branch (never straight to `main`) via rebase, promoted to `main` at milestone boundaries gated on that milestone's integration-test suite passing. App-lock and privacy overlay are wired in rough-but-real from the very first task, not deferred to a later "safety" milestone, so every later milestone's manual verification happens inside the real trust envelope. Sequencing follows the already-locked critical path: infra/domain/data seam first, then capture, then detail/safety in parallel, then export+paywall, then monetization/widget/hardening.

```tasks-json

{"id": "T-001", "title": "Project scaffold: XcodeGen project.yml (app + widget extension + unit/UI test bundle targets), folder skeleton matching lane ownership — pbxproj is generated only, never hand-edited", "owner_lane": "data_domain", "files": ["project.yml", "Provenance.xcodeproj", "Domain/", "Data/", "Services/", "Features/", "Polish/", "Widget/", "Tests/"], "depends_on": [], "acceptance_criteria": ["xcodegen generate produces a project that builds an empty app + empty widget target", "Folder skeleton matches the four-lane ownership map exactly, no stray top-level files", "project.pbxproj is generated from project.yml; no lane hand-edits it directly", "Domain/, Services/, Data/, Features/, Polish/, Widget/ created as empty directory skeletons only — this task does not claim content ownership of files placed inside them by other tasks"], "status": "pending"},

{"id": "T-003", "title": "Pure display-state and partition functions with exhaustive unit tests", "owner_lane": "data_domain", "files": ["Domain/DisplayState/ItemDisplayState.swift", "Domain/DisplayState/ComputeDisplayState.swift", "Domain/DisplayState/PartitionActiveAndLocked.swift", "Domain/CollectionSummary.swift", "Domain/ExportMode.swift", "Tests/DomainTests/DisplayStateTests.swift", "Tests/DomainTests/PartitionTests.swift"], "depends_on": ["T-002"], "acceptance_criteria": ["computeDisplayState precedence verified: deleted > locked > incomplete > normal", "partitionActiveAndLocked is O(n), tested with tied creationSequence values", "restore-at-cap and delete-of-locked-item-promotes-next-oldest covered by parametrized tests", "zero UI or SwiftData dependency in these files"], "status": "pending"},
