# provenance — Final Review

_Generated by the autonomous multi-agent orchestrator on 2026-07-06 23:16:34._

## Original Prompt

```
PORTFOLIO_CHILD_PROJECT: true
Parent portfolio: multi-app-exp4
Selected app slug: provenance

Build this app as a working local-first SwiftUI MVP.

## Selected App Spec

# Provenance

Build mode: **MVP build**.
Build priority: **1**.

## App Name

Provenance

## Category

Collecting & Valuation

## One-Sentence Promise

Photograph what you collect and get insurance-grade condition, provenance, and value tracking without a spreadsheet.

## Target User

Serious collectors of high-value physical goods (watches, vinyl, trading cards, wine, sneakers) who currently track holdings in Notes or spreadsheets and have no real sense of aggregate value or condition history.

## Painful Moment Solved

You just bought or received a piece, you want to log it before the details slip your mind, and existing apps are single-category or spreadsheet-grade.

## Why It Is Different

Cross-category, condition-aware valuation over time with photographic condition history, and an actual exportable report insurers/appraisers can use.

## Why Users Would Pay

Ongoing market-value tracking and alerts, unlimited items/categories, and insurance-ready PDF export are gated behind the paid tier.

## Subscription Model

Free: manual catalog up to 25 items, no valuation tracking. Paid (~$6.99/mo or $59/yr): unlimited items, on-device photo-based item ID assist, value-over-time charts, insurance export.

## Local-First MVP Scope

SwiftData-backed local catalog, on-device Vision framework classification assist, manual condition/provenance fields, local value-history entries, PDF export via local rendering.

## Future Backend Roadmap

Repository protocol boundary now; later, optional cloud sync and an opt-in market-price feed behind the same interface.

## AR / Local ML / Local LLM Fit

On-device Vision-based item ID assist is integral to reducing cataloging friction; fully usable via manual entry if classification fails.

## Design Direction

Quiet, archival, museum-catalog aesthetic — dark neutral backgrounds, serif accents, photo-forward cards.

## App Store Positioning

The valuation ledger for people who collect things worth tracking.

## Invalidation Criteria

If users only ever use manual entry and value fields go unfilled, reposition as pure catalog+condition tracking.

## v1 Build Scope

Single-user local catalog, photo capture + classification assist, condition/provenance logging, manual value entries with history chart, PDF insurance export, full state coverage, StoreKit 2 paywall.

## Core Workflows

- capture item via photo
- assist-classify item type/model
- log condition, provenance, purchase price
- track value entries over time
- generate insurance export report

## iOS-Native Features

- Vision framework on-device classification
- Camera capture
- Files/document export
- Widgets for total collection value
- Dynamic Type/VoiceOver accessible detail views

## Key Risks

- On-device classification may misidentify niche items
- Value tracking depends on user-entered market data absent a live price feed

## Claude Design Handoff Prompt

Design a premium iOS collector's valuation app called Provenance. Archival/museum-catalog aesthetic, dark neutral background, serif display type, photo-forward item cards, condition timeline view, value-over-time chart screen. Include empty state, item detail screen, insurance-export preview.

## Parent Portfolio Prompt

Build 7 completely separate iOS apps at the same time.

Requirements:
1. Each app must go from 0 to production-ready, not just a few features.
2. Each app must be unique, useful, and commercially viable.
3. Each app must be beautifully designed and feel premium.
4. Each app must provide real end-user value.
5. Each app should have a realistic path to monetization, with a subscription or paid offering that offers genuine value.
6. Each app should have viral potential in general or within a niche, but virality must not come at the expense of usefulness.
7. Each app should be better than its competitors in a meaningful way.
8. Each app should be local-first, but architected so cloud support could be added later without rewriting everything.
9. The seven apps must not be similar to each other.
10. No app can be similar to anything I have already built before — including the apps already in this workspace (e.g. Waylay and TrueScale, currently in progress).
11. Do not reuse prior concepts, themes, or mechanics from earlier apps unless there is a strong, clearly justified reason.

Bonus points:
- Unique use of LLMs
- Unique use of AR
- Unique use of ML

Important:
- Bonus points are optional. Do not force them in if they would weaken the app.
- If an LLM, AR, or ML feature is used, it must provide real value and be integral to the product.
- Do not bias the ideas toward bonus points.
- Do not make the app around novelty alone.
- The final result for each app must be a full production-ready app, not a prototype.

Build rules:
- Run the seven app efforts in parallel.
- Keep the discussion and design phases separate for each app.
- Each app should get its own full product direction, design direction, architecture, implementation plan, and build output.
- Save all prompt discussion, phase discussion, and final decisions for each app.
- At the end, combine the full transcript for each app into a .txt file.
- If an app is liked, also prepare a Jira board and Notion project for it separately, including epics, tasks, fields, and all the implementation structure needed for backfilling later.
- If the system supports multiple rounds per phase, use enough rounds to get high-quality discussion, but allow a phase to end early if consensus is reached.
- Make the orchestrator resilient: if a phase stalls, recover cleanly instead of looping.
- Report only important milestones, not every tiny step.

Output:
- Create one folder per app in the output directory.
- There must be one folder per app, not one folder containing all apps.
- Keep the outputs clearly separated and organized.

Output directory:
- /Users/pchordia/Documents/iOS-App-Factory

Now generate candidate app ideas (at least 10), discuss them thoroughly, choose the best 7, and build all seven to production quality.
```

## Phase Purpose

Final review of completeness, consistency, risks, quality gates, and a clear go / no-go decision.

## Transcript


### Round 1


**Codex — Round 1 (skipped: CLI unavailable)**

_Codex skipped — in cooldown (rate_limit)._

**Claude (Delivery Lead · Pragmatist) — Round 1**

This has actually reached a real terminus, and I want to call it what it is rather than dress it up: this is a **build-complete, submission-blocked** app, and the honest move here is to stop generating more discussion phases and instead hand back a short, owned punch list.

Here's my read on sequencing. The build itself cleared its bar — compiles cleanly for the iOS Simulator after three real repair rounds, all 25 tasks from task_assignments are present and wired, the two genuine logic bugs that got found (entitlement offline-collapse, orphaned test directories) got fixed with actual diffs, not just descriptions of fixes. That's a real, verifiable milestone and I don't think final_review should relitigate it. But "compiles" and "ready to hand to a user" are two different bars, and this project has three concrete gaps between them that are each small, each owned by "whoever has hands on the actual repo" (not this sandbox), and each blocking in a different way — so they shouldn't be flattened into one vague "needs more polish" line.

Gap one is test coverage, and it's a scope gap, not a build gap: four of six locked acceptance-criteria test files were never written (SafetyIntegrityIntegrationTests, ExportVerificationIntegrationTests, StoreKitScenarioIntegrationTests, FullRegressionIntegrationTests). That means the export pipeline and the entire StoreKit purchase/restore/downgrade flow — the two subsystems this app's monetization and its core "we're honest with your data" promise both depend on — have zero automated coverage. The human_qa_checklist exists specifically because of this hole, and it's a legitimate stopgap, but a human running a checklist once is not the same as CI catching a regression on the next change. I'd sequence this as: write StoreKitScenarioIntegrationTests first (SKTestSession harness), since that's the subsystem with a documented history of a real silent-failure bug in this exact build, then the export matrix test, then wire FullRegressionIntegrationTests last since it depends on the others.

Gap two is App Store submission readiness, and this is where I think the room already did the hard thinking and just needs someone to go execute against real files instead of inferring from transcripts: confirm PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy actually exists (named as a tracked task in tech_specs, never confirmed built anywhere after), confirm the paywall's legal footer has real hosted URLs not placeholders (Guideline 3.1.2 is a near-certain rejection if missing), and scrub "insurance" from anything that will actually reach App Store Connect. None of this is a design decision — it's a checklist someone runs against the real Xcode project in an environment that isn't sandboxed away from the filesystem.

Gap three is the one I'd actually lose sleep over as a delivery lead, because it's a trust-model risk, not paperwork: the widget lock-screen value leak and the backup/restore cycle have never been observed, not "tested and found wanting" — literally never run. Both map onto the two claims this entire product is sold on (Face ID protects your valuables list; your only copy isn't at risk without cloud sync). If either of those turns out to be broken, that's not a bug-fix ticket, it's a redesign of the widget or the backup messaging, and I'd rather this room say plainly "we do not know if the core trust promise holds" than let a downstream reader assume "QA'd, ship it" because the build says VERIFIED.

My biggest objection to my own framing: I'm about to recommend a "NOT READY, do these three things" verdict for the fourth time in a row across app_store_readiness and now here — at some point that stops being caution and starts being a room that never actually closes anything because nobody with real device/file access ever shows up to execute. If this phase just restates the same three gaps with the same "someone should check" hand-wave, that's not final review, that's a fourth opinion on the same unexecuted to-do list. So I think final_review's job is narrower than repeating the gaps: it's to say, in one place, exactly what's done (compiles, full feature set, core loop verified functionally by one reviewer's manual reasoning), exactly what's unverified-not-broken (the four test files, the App Store paperwork, the two trust behaviors), and to stop short of inventing new work — because there isn't any new work to invent, there's just execution debt against tasks already named by name in this same transcript.

For what has to survive into the final output: the distinction between "VERIFIED" (build/compile status, which is real and earned) and "launch ready" (which is not true yet) has to stay sharp — conflating those two is the single biggest way this review could mislead someone into shipping. And the three gaps need to stay named with their exact artifacts (the four test file names, PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy, the widget/backup checks) rather than summarized into "needs more testing," because vague follow-ups are how this exact set of gaps survived four phases without anyone closing them.

**Gemini — Round 1 (skipped: CLI unavailable)**

_Gemini unavailable headless (agy needs a terminal; gemini-cli tier deprecated). gemini CLI -> auth/tier error_

**Coordinator (Claude) — decision after round 1**

Here's the state of play: this round was Claude flying solo again — Codex and Gemini both stayed dark the entire build, which has been the pattern since day one. But she used the round well. Instead of just re-summarizing prior phases, she made an explicit argument about what *this* phase's job actually is: not to relitigate whether the build is good, but to draw a hard, unambiguous line between "compiles cleanly" (true, earned, verified three times over) and "ready to hand to a user" (not true yet), and to name the remaining gaps as concrete, owned artifacts rather than vague "needs more testing" hand-waving. She also flagged her own risk directly — that this is the fourth phase in a row to say "not ready, do these three things," and that if final_review just repeats that without adding clarity, it's not doing its job. Since nobody contested or added anything (there's no one else in the room), and her output already satisfies everything the phase playbook asks for — what was built, verification status, prompt coverage, known limitations, follow-ups with next steps — I'm calling this converged rather than waiting on a round that isn't coming.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**What was built:** A complete, wired Provenance MVP covering all 25 tasks from `task_assignments` — capture-to-save under a minute with silent, non-blocking Vision classification; full item detail with three separated modules (purchase facts, condition/provenance timeline, value ledger); soft-delete with undo toast and recovery; the computed active/locked 25-item partition with correct promotion-on-delete and uncapped restore; Face ID/passcode app-lock with the no-passcode skip case; local off-main-thread PDF export (single-item and collection) with a native paged, VoiceOver-accessible preview; StoreKit 2 paywall with a real redacted export mock; and a widget with free/paid states. Two genuine logic bugs found during build verification were fixed with real diffs, not just described: the entitlement provider's silent collapse-to-`.free` on an untrusted/offline app transaction, and two previously-orphaned test directories that weren't even wired into the Xcode project.

**Verification status:** VERIFIED — compiled cleanly for the iOS Simulator via `xcodebuild`, after 3 repair rounds (an `@Observable` conformance fix, a Swift 6 strict-concurrency capture fix, and a widget Info.plist bundle-identifier fix). This is real, earned, compiler-confirmed status — but it is a compile/run verdict, not a "ready to ship" verdict, and the two should not be conflated.

**Prompt coverage:** Every hard requirement from the original spec and the locked `prompt_contract` is present in the build — SwiftData local-first catalog, Vision-assist classification, condition/provenance/value tracking, PDF export, StoreKit 2 paywall matching the stated free/paid model, repository-protocol boundary for future cloud sync, widget, and Dynamic Type/VoiceOver support. The "insurance-grade" language from the original prompt was deliberately never carried into product copy per the locked decision that the export is professional and structured, not a legal/insurance claim.

**Known limitations (named precisely, not vaguely):**
1. Four of six locked acceptance-criteria test files were never written: `SafetyIntegrityIntegrationTests.swift`, `ExportVerificationIntegrationTests.swift`, `StoreKitScenarioIntegrationTests.swift`, `FullRegressionIntegrationTests.swift` — meaning the export pipeline and the entire StoreKit purchase/restore/downgrade flow have zero automated regression coverage; a human QA script is the only current safety net there.
2. App Store submission paperwork is unconfirmed against the real files: whether `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` actually exists (named as a tracked task in `tech_specs`, never confirmed built afterward), and whether the paywall's legal footer has real hosted Privacy Policy/Terms URLs rather than placeholders (a near-certain Guideline 3.1.2 rejection if missing).
3. Two core trust-model behaviors have never been observed running at all, not just tested and found wanting: whether the paid widget leaks the real collection-value total on a locked screen with zero authentication, and whether a real device backup/restore cycle actually preserves the full catalog. Both map directly to the product's central promises (Face ID protects your valuables list; your only copy isn't at risk without cloud sync) — if either is broken, that's a design fix, not a bug ticket.

**Top follow-ups (each with a concrete next step, not just a name):**
- Write `StoreKitScenarioIntegrationTests.swift` first (needs a real `SKTestSession` harness) — highest priority since this exact subsystem already had one real silent-failure bug in this build.
- Then `ExportVerificationIntegrationTests.swift`, then `FullRegressionIntegrationTests.swift` last (it depends on the others existing).
- Run the App Store paperwork checklist against the actual Xcode project in a non-sandboxed environment: confirm `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy`, confirm real legal URLs on the paywall, scrub any "insurance" language from submission copy.
- Physically run the widget-lock-screen check and one real backup/restore cycle before shipping — these are go/no-go checks on the product's core trust claims, not deferrable QA line items.

This is a build-complete, submission-blocked app: strong, verifiable engineering work with a short, named, executable punch list standing between it and a real App Store submission.

**Quality Gate (Claude) — after round 1**

```
Free: manual catalog up to 25 items, no valuation tracking. Paid (~$6.99/mo or $59/yr): unlimited items, on-device photo-based item ID assist, value-over-time charts, insurance export.

## Local-First MVP Scope

SwiftData-backed local catalog, on-device Vision framework classification assist, manual condition/provenance fields, local value-history entries, PDF export via local rendering.

## Future Backend Roadmap

Repository protocol boundary now; later, optional cloud sync and an opt-in market-price feed behind the same interface.

## AR / Local ML / Local LLM Fit

On-device Vision-based item ID assist is integral to reducing cataloging friction; fully usable via manual entry if classification fails.

## Design Direction

Quiet, archival, museum-catalog aesthetic — dark neutral backgrounds, serif accents, photo-forward cards.

## App Store Positioning

The valuation ledger for people who collect things worth tracking.

## Invalidation Criteria

If users only ever use manual entry and value fields go unfilled, reposition as pure catalog+condition tracking.

## v1 Build Scope

Single-user local catalog, photo capture + classification assist, condition/provenance logging, manual value entries with history chart, PDF insurance export, full state coverage, StoreKit 2 paywall.

## Core Workflows

- capture item via photo
- assist-classify item type/model
- log condition, provenance, purchase price
- track value entries over time
- generate insurance export report

## iOS-Native Features

- Vision framework on-device classification
- Camera capture
- Files/document export
- Widgets for total collection value
- Dynamic Type/VoiceOver accessible detail views

## Key Risks

- On-device classification may misidentify niche items
- Value tracking depends on user-entered market data absent a live price feed

## Claude Design Handoff Prompt

Design a premium iOS collector's valuation app called Provenance. Archival/museum-catalog aesthetic, dark neutral background, serif display type, photo-forward item cards, condition timeline view, value-over-time chart screen. Include empty state, item detail screen, insurance-export preview.

## Parent Portfolio Prompt

Build 7 completely separate iOS apps at the same time.

Requirements:
1. Each app must go from 0 to production-ready, not just a few features.
2. Each app must be unique, useful, and commercially viable.
3. Each app must be beautifully designed and feel premium.
4. Each app must provide real end-user value.
5. Each app should have a realistic path to monetization, with a subscription or paid offering that offers genuine value.
6. Each app should have viral potential in general or within a niche, but virality must not come at the expense of usefulness.
7. Each app should be better than its competitors in a meaningful way.
8. Each app should be local-first, but architected so cloud support could be added later without rewriting everything.
9. The seven apps must not be similar to each other.
10. No app can be similar to anything I have already built before — including the apps already in this workspace (e.g. Waylay and TrueScale, currently in progress).
11. Do not reuse prior concepts, themes, or mechanics from earlier apps unless there is a strong, clearly justified reason.

Bonus points:
- Unique use of LLMs
- Unique use of AR
- Unique use of ML

Important:
- Bonus points are optional. Do not force them in if they would weaken the app.
- If an LLM, AR, or ML feature is used, it must provide real value and be integral to the product.
- Do not bias the ideas toward bonus points.
- Do not make the app around novelty alone.
- The final result for each app must be a full production-ready app, not a prototype.

Build rules:
- Run the seven app efforts in parallel.
- Keep the discussion and design phases separate for each app.
- Each app should get its own full product direction, design direction, architecture, implementation plan, and build output.
- Save all prompt discussion, phase discussion, and final decisions for each app.
- At the end, combine the full transcript for each app into a .txt file.
- If an app is liked, also prepare a Jira board and Notion project for it separately, including epics, tasks, fields, and all the implementation structure needed for backfilling later.
- If the system supports multiple rounds per phase, use enough rounds to get high-quality discussion, but allow a phase to end early if consensus is reached.
- Make the orchestrator resilient: if a phase stalls, recover cleanly instead of looping.
- Report only important milestones, not every tiny step.

Output:
- Create one folder per app in the output directory.
- There must be one folder per app, not one folder containing all apps.
- Keep the outputs clearly separated and organized.

Output directory:
- /Users/pchordia/Documents/iOS-App-Factory

Now generate candidate app ideas (at least 10), discuss them thoroughly, choose the best 7, and build all seven to production quality.
```

## Phase Purpose

For multi-app prompts, audit the portfolio shape: every requested app is a sibling folder, every selected app has the right workflow, child prompts preserve the parent requirements, and no category was silently collapsed. For single-app prompts, record that portfolio audit is not applicable.

## Transcript


### Round 1


**Codex — Round 1 (skipped: CLI unavailable)**

_Codex skipped — in cooldown (rate_limit)._

**Claude (QA & Risk · User Advocate) — Round 1**

Looking at this phase's actual job — audit the portfolio shape — the honest starting point is the same one every earlier phase in this transcript already established: this room was handed exactly one app, Provenance, as a child of a `multi-app-exp4` parent that already ran the 7-app selection somewhere else. So the comparative audit (did we get 7 folders, were any of the 7 categories silently merged, does every sibling have the right workflow) is not something this phase can actually perform — I don't have visibility into the other six app folders or the parent orchestrator's manifest from this sandbox, only into what happened to Provenance itself. I want to say that plainly rather than manufacture a false confirmation that "the portfolio looks fine," because I literally can't see the other six.

What I can audit, and where I'd actually apply pressure: does Provenance's own slice of this look like a properly-isolated child project, not bleeding into or duplicating parent-level concerns? Reading back through every phase, yes — nobody in 15+ phases ever tried to re-run idea generation, re-select apps, or pull in adjacent categories, and `portfolio_selection` explicitly rejected doing so for good reasons (contradicts the child-project marker, would violate one-folder-per-app, would fracture the cross-category promise that's this app's whole differentiator). The category "Collecting & Valuation" stayed intact end to end — nothing about wine/watches/cards/vinyl/sneakers got split into per-category sub-apps or silently dropped. That's a real, verifiable finding, not a rubber stamp.

Here's the thing I think actually deserves scrutiny in this phase, though, and nobody has touched it in 21 prior phases: the parent prompt has an explicit instruction — "if an app is liked, also prepare a Jira board and Notion project for it separately, including epics, tasks, fields, and all the implementation structure needed for backfilling later." Provenance clearly qualifies as "liked" — it went through the full pipeline to a VERIFIED, build-complete state with a final_review that called it ship-worthy modulo a punch list. But there is no mention anywhere in this entire transcript of a Jira board or a Notion project being prepared for it. Not a partial attempt, not a "deferred to a later phase" note — total silence. If the parent orchestrator is relying on each child's phases to signal when backfill prep is needed, that signal never fired here, and if it's relying on this exact portfolio_audit phase to catch that gap, then this is the moment to say it out loud rather than let it slide through as an implicit non-issue. I'd treat this as a real backfill-readiness gap: Provenance has more than enough structure already sitting in `task_assignments` (25 named tasks with owner lanes, dependencies, acceptance criteria) to backfill into Jira epics/tickets directly, and `app_features`/`project_plan` map cleanly onto Notion project fields — the raw material exists, but nobody has been tasked with actually standing up those boards, and no phase in this pipeline is named for that job.

The other thing I'd flag rather than assume away: I have no way to confirm from here that Provenance actually lives in its own sibling folder alongside six others (not nested inside a shared wrapper, not accidentally merged with another app's output directory). Every earlier phase that touched this question reasoned about it from the text of the prompt and prior decisions, never from an actual `ls` on `/Users/pchordia/Documents/iOS-App-Factory`. Given this build's whole pattern of "claimed done" vs. "actually verified" gaps (the compiler-unavailable saga, the never-run backup/restore cycle, the unconfirmed privacy manifest), I don't think it's safe to assume the folder structure is correct just because nobody flagged it as wrong — absence of a complaint isn't confirmation. If whoever runs the parent-level audit has real filesystem access, that's a five-second check that should actually happen rather than being inferred.

So my read: portfolio audit is not applicable as a comparative exercise at this per-app level, and everything internally consistent about Provenance's own category/scope/workflow checks out clean. But I don't want that "not applicable" to quietly swallow two real, checkable things — the missing Jira/Notion backfill artifacts for a liked app, and the never-verified sibling-folder placement — that this phase is exactly positioned to surface and hand upward. Those are the two items I'd want preserved word-for-word into whatever final output closes this phase, not softened into "portfolio structure appears fine."

**Gemini — Round 1 (skipped: CLI unavailable)**

_Gemini unavailable headless (agy needs a terminal; gemini-cli tier deprecated). gemini CLI -> auth/tier error_

**Coordinator (Claude) — decision after round 1**

CONSENSUS: YES

Here's where this landed: only Claude actually weighed in (Codex and Gemini stayed dark, same as basically every phase in this build), but the analysis holds up on its own merits and directly answers what this phase is supposed to check. The core call is straightforward — this room was handed exactly one child app (Provenance) inside a `multi-app-exp4` parent that already did the 7-app selection somewhere upstream, so there's no sibling-app comparison to actually perform from inside this sandbox. That's not a cop-out, it's the same conclusion every earlier phase (`portfolio_selection` especially) already reached, and nothing here reopens it.

What's useful is that the audit didn't just stop at "not applicable" — it surfaced two concrete things worth handing upward rather than letting them evaporate into a shrug:

1. **Jira/Notion backfill was never done.** The parent prompt explicitly says liked apps get a Jira board + Notion project prepared separately, with epics/tasks/fields for backfilling. Provenance clearly qualifies as "liked" — it went all the way to a VERIFIED, build-complete state — but there's no mention anywhere in 20+ phases of that backfill actually happening. The raw material to do it exists (25 tasks with owners/dependencies/acceptance criteria in `task_assignments`, feature/plan docs that map cleanly to Notion fields), it just never got executed.
2. **Sibling-folder placement was never actually verified on disk.** Every earlier phase reasoned about "one folder per app" from the text of decisions, never from an actual filesystem check. Given this build's track record of "claimed done" vs. "actually verified" gaps, that's worth a real `ls` from whoever has parent-level filesystem access, not an assumption.

Nothing contested here since there's one voice in the room, but the findings are concrete and checkable, not hand-wavy, so I'm treating this as closed.

## Final Output

**Portfolio applicability decision:** Not applicable as a comparative audit. This is a single-app child project (`PORTFOLIO_CHILD_PROJECT: true`, `Selected app slug: provenance`) inside the `multi-app-exp4` parent, which already ran idea generation and the 7-app selection upstream. This phase has no visibility into the other six sibling app folders or the parent's manifest, so it cannot and should not attempt to validate the full 7-app portfolio shape — that check belongs to a parent-level audit with real access across all seven folders.

**Expected vs. actual app/project count:** Not verifiable from here. One child (Provenance) is confirmed complete end-to-end within this sandbox; whether six other sibling folders exist with the same completeness is outside this phase's visibility and must be confirmed by the parent orchestrator.

**Child folder/workflow check:** Provenance has a valid initial prompt (the full spec embedded in `initial_prompt.md`) and ran the complete workflow — prompt_contract through final_review, all 22 phases — with no evidence of scope drift, re-running portfolio ceremony, or bleeding into parent-level concerns. This is a clean, correctly-isolated child project.

**Skipped or collapsed category warnings:** None for Provenance's own category ("Collecting & Valuation"). It was never split into per-category sub-apps (watches/wine/cards/vinyl/sneakers stayed unified under one cross-category schema, which is the app's core differentiator) and never merged with an adjacent category. Whether any of the *other six* categories in the parent portfolio were silently collapsed or dropped is unknown and unverifiable from this vantage point.

**Backfill readiness notes for Jira/Notion:** **Gap identified.** The parent prompt requires a Jira board and Notion project (epics, tasks, fields, full implementation structure) for any app that's "liked" — Provenance clearly qualifies, having reached a VERIFIED, build-complete state through final_review. No phase in this entire pipeline shows that backfill work happening. The material to do it exists and is ready to use: `task_assignments`' 25 tasks (with owner lanes, dependencies, acceptance criteria) map directly to Jira epics/tickets, and `app_features`/`project_plan` map cleanly to Notion project fields. This should be flagged to the parent orchestrator as outstanding, unassigned work — not silently treated as done or as out of scope.

**Additional unverified item flagged for parent-level follow-up:** No phase in this build ever confirmed via actual filesystem access that Provenance sits in its own sibling folder under `/Users/pchordia/Documents/iOS-App-Factory` alongside six others, rather than nested in or merged with a shared wrapper. Given this build's recurring pattern of assumed-vs-verified gaps, this warrants a direct check by whoever has real filesystem access at the parent level.

**Quality Gate (Claude) — after round 1**

QUALITY: PASS

## Feedback

This closes cleanly. The output correctly executes the phase-rule branch for single-app runs ("explicitly mark the audit not applicable instead of inventing portfolio work") while still producing real, checkable content rather than a bare shrug:

- **Portfolio applicability decision**: correctly scoped — single child app inside `multi-app-exp4`, no fabricated comparison to six sibling apps this sandbox can't see.
- **Expected vs. actual count**: honestly stated as unverifiable from this vantage point rather than assumed.
- **Child folder/workflow check**: concrete and grounded — valid `initial_prompt.md`, full 22-phase pipeline run with no scope drift or re-run of parent-level ceremony (matches `portfolio_selection`'s own prior reasoning, doesn't contradict it).
- **Skipped/collapsed category warnings**: correctly scoped to what's checkable (Provenance's own category stayed unified, cross-category schema intact) with an honest disclaimer about the other six.
- **Backfill readiness notes**: this is the real value-add of the phase — it caught a genuine, previously-unmentioned gap (parent prompt's Jira/Notion backfill requirement for "liked" apps, never executed anywhere in 20+ phases) and ties it to concrete existing artifacts (`task_assignments`' 25 tasks, `app_features`/`project_plan`) rather than vague "should probably do this."

It also adds a legitimate, non-redundant finding (sibling-folder placement never verified via actual filesystem access) consistent with this build's recurring "claimed vs. verified" pattern flagged in `build_verification`/`app_store_readiness`/`final_review` — this is thematically consistent, not a new tangent.

No contradiction with any locked decision, no invented portfolio work, both real gaps recorded as explicit flags for parent-level follow-up rather than buried. Single-reviewer process caveat is consistent with the rest of this build and doesn't block closing given the findings are concrete and independently defensible.


## Interface Contracts

```json
{
  "interfaces": [
    {
      "name": "ItemCategory",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "Swift",
      "signature": "enum ItemCategory: String, Codable, CaseIterable { case watch, vinyl, card, wine, sneaker, other }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Fixed six-category enum shared across capture, detail, export, and classification mapping."
    },
    {
      "name": "ItemStatus",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "enum ItemStatus: String, Codable, Sendable { case draft, incomplete, complete }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Persisted creation/completion state; separate from computed ItemDisplayState."
    },
    {
      "name": "ConditionGrade",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "enum ConditionGrade: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Comparable, Sendable { case poor, fair, good, veryGood, excellent, mint }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Ordered enum (not free text) so condition-change comparisons over time are meaningful."
    },
    {
      "name": "ValueType",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "enum ValueType: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case purchasePrice, appraisal, dealerOffer, marketObservation, insuredValue }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Hard constrained enum; ledger/chart rendering must visually distinguish each case, never one undifferentiated line."
    },
    {
      "name": "PhotoAssetRef",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct PhotoAssetRef: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { let originalRelativePath: String; let thumbnailRelativePath: String?; let pixelWidth: Int; let pixelHeight: Int }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Settled over a bare String path -- stored dimensions let export decide downsample-vs-embed without re-decoding. All photo fields use this type."
    },
    {
      "name": "PurchaseFacts",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct PurchaseFacts: Codable, Hashable, Sendable { var purchaseDate: Date?; var purchaseAmount: Decimal?; var source: String?; var locationNote: String? }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Decimal, not minor-unit Int, for every monetary value app-wide -- no parallel cents-convention to remember at each call site."
    },
    {
      "name": "Item",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "@Model final class Item { @Attribute(.unique) var id: UUID; var title: String; var category: Category; var subtype: String; var status: ItemStatus; var createdAt: Date; var creationSequence: Int64; var deletedAt: Date?; var coverPhoto: PhotoAssetRef?; var conditionGrade: ConditionGrade?; var purchaseFacts: PurchaseFacts?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var conditionEntries: [ConditionEntry]; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade) var valueEntries: [ValueEntry] }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "creationSequence is the mandatory stable tiebreak for active-25 partition; never sort on createdAt alone."
    },
    {
      "name": "ConditionEntry",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "@Model final class ConditionEntry { @Attribute(.unique) var id: UUID; var itemID: UUID; var entryDate: Date; var note: String; var provenanceNote: String?; var conditionChange: ConditionGrade?; var photo: PhotoAssetRef? }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "One photo max per entry, per locked scope."
    },
    {
      "name": "ValueEntry",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "@Model final class ValueEntry { @Attribute(.unique) var id: UUID; var itemID: UUID; var entryDate: Date; var amount: Decimal; var valueType: ValueType; var sourceNote: String?; var photo: PhotoAssetRef? }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Decimal amount, never Double, to avoid float drift in a financial ledger."
    },
    {
      "name": "AppSettings",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "@Model final class AppSettings { @Attribute(.unique) var id: Int; var installCurrencyCode: String; var appLockEnabled: Bool; var recentlyDeletedRetentionDays: Int; var installedAt: Date }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Singleton row (id fixed at 1); currencyCode stamped once at install from Locale.current, never re-derived."
    },
    {
      "name": "EntitlementSnapshot",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "Swift",
      "signature": "struct EntitlementSnapshot: Codable, Equatable { var isProActive: Bool; var lastVerifiedAt: Date? }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "Cached last-known capability state for offline continuity."
    },
    {
      "name": "ItemDisplayState",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "enum ItemDisplayState: Equatable, Sendable { case normal; case incomplete(reason: String); case locked; case deleted(purgeDate: Date) }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Associated-value shape so badges/VoiceOver strings build directly from the enum with no second lookup. Precedence: deleted > locked > incomplete > normal."
    },
    {
      "name": "CollectionSummary",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct CollectionSummary: Equatable, Sendable { let activeItemCount: Int; let lockedItemCount: Int; let totalCollectionValue: Decimal?; let currencyCode: String; let lastComputedAt: Date }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Single source of truth for widget and any in-app aggregate value display; never computed twice independently."
    },
    {
      "name": "ClassificationSuggestion",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct ClassificationSuggestion: Sendable, Equatable { let category: Category; let confidence: Double; let sourceLabel: String }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "sourceLabel retained for auditing the Vision-label-to-Category mapping table; nil/absent entirely below the confidence floor."
    },
    {
      "name": "ExportMode",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "enum ExportMode: Sendable { case singleItem(UUID); case collection }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Declared once here, imported everywhere -- no per-lane redeclaration."
    },
    {
      "name": "ExportPagePreview",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct ExportPagePreview: Equatable, Sendable { let pageIndex: Int; let totalPages: Int; let imageFileURL: URL }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "Carries page-position metadata directly so 'Page 2 of 5' VoiceOver announcement is explicit and testable, not inferred from a bare image array."
    },
    {
      "name": "ItemRepository",
      "kind": "protocol",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "protocol ItemRepository: Sendable { func createDraft(coverPhoto: PhotoAssetRef?) async throws -> Item; func updateQuickLog(itemID: UUID, title: String, category: Category, subtype: String) async throws -> Item; func markItemIncomplete(itemID: UUID, reason: String) async throws; func loadActiveAndLocked(subscriptionActive: Bool) async throws -> (active: [Item], locked: [Item]); func loadItemDetail(itemID: UUID) async throws -> Item; func savePurchaseFacts(itemID: UUID, facts: PurchaseFacts?) async throws; func addConditionEntry(_ entry: ConditionEntry) async throws; func addValueEntry(_ entry: ValueEntry) async throws; func softDelete(_ itemID: UUID, deletedAt: Date) async throws; func restore(_ itemID: UUID) async throws; func purgeExpiredDeletedItems(now: Date) async throws; func loadRecentlyDeletedItems(now: Date) async throws -> [Item]; func collectionSummary(subscriptionActive: Bool) async throws -> CollectionSummary }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Canonical persistence boundary, one method set. loadActiveAndLocked MUST delegate to partitionActiveAndLocked rather than reimplement the split -- closes the two-independently-written-truths risk."
    },
    {
      "name": "PhotoStorageService",
      "kind": "protocol",
      "language": "Swift",
      "signature": "protocol PhotoStorageService { func persistOriginalAndThumbnail(imageData: Data, itemID: UUID) async throws -> PhotoAssetRef; func persistEntryPhoto(imageData: Data, itemID: UUID, entryID: UUID) async throws -> PhotoAssetRef; func removeAssets(forItemID itemID: UUID) async throws; func removeAssets(forEntryID entryID: UUID, itemID: UUID) async throws; func makePreviewImage(for asset: PhotoAssetRef) async throws -> Data }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "Handles App Group file layout, thumbnail generation, and cleanup."
    },
    {
      "name": "ClassificationService",
      "kind": "protocol",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "protocol ClassificationService: Sendable { func classify(imageData: Data) async -> ClassificationSuggestion? }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "VNClassifyImageRequest-backed impl maps Vision labels to Category via a reviewed lookup-table artifact, not an inline switch."
    },
    {
      "name": "EntitlementProvider",
      "kind": "protocol",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "protocol EntitlementProvider: Sendable { var current: EntitlementState { get async }; func purchase(_ product: Product) async throws; func restorePurchases() async throws; func startTransactionListener() async }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "Gates from Transaction.currentEntitlements (local cache), never a live network call. Listener started once at app launch."
    },
    {
      "name": "AppLockService",
      "kind": "protocol",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "protocol AppLockService: Sendable { func deviceAuthenticationAvailable() -> Bool; func authenticate() async -> Bool }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "If device authentication is unavailable, access is allowed rather than trapping the user."
    },
    {
      "name": "ExportService",
      "kind": "protocol",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "protocol ExportService: Sendable { func buildDocumentModel(mode: ExportMode) async throws -> ExportDocumentModel; func renderPDF(_ document: ExportDocumentModel) async throws -> URL; func generatePreviewPages(for pdfURL: URL) async throws -> [ExportPagePreview]; func cleanupTemporaryExportFiles() async }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "Real file rendered via UIGraphicsPDFRenderer off-main; preview generated separately from the file, never via PDFView. buildDocumentModel is the one mapping seam."
    },
    {
      "name": "computeDisplayState",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "func computeDisplayState(item: Item, subscriptionActive: Bool, activeItemIDs: Set<UUID>) -> ItemDisplayState",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Pure function. Exhaustively unit tested (Swift Testing @Test(arguments:)) including tied creationSequence, restore-at-cap, delete-while-locked."
    },
    {
      "name": "partitionActiveAndLocked",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "func partitionActiveAndLocked(items: [Item], freeCapActive: Bool, capSize: Int) -> (active: [Item], locked: [Item])",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "O(n) single pass: filter non-deleted, sort by creationSequence, split at capSize. This is the ONLY place the active/locked split is computed -- ItemRepository.loadActiveAndLocked must delegate here, never reimplement it as an independent query."
    },
    {
      "name": "CaptureWorkflowState",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "Swift",
      "signature": "enum CaptureWorkflowState: Equatable { case idle; case draftCreated(Item); case mediaProcessing(Item); case suggestionReady(Item, ClassificationSuggestion?); case saveReady(Item, ClassificationSuggestion?); case failedIncomplete(Item) }",
      "owning_lane": "primary_ui",
      "notes": "Single feature-model state machine for capture and quick log."
    },
    {
      "name": "CatalogRoute",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "enum CatalogRoute: Hashable, Sendable { case itemDetail(UUID); case preservedList; case recentlyDeleted; case exportPreview(ExportMode); case settings }",
      "owning_lane": "primary_ui",
      "notes": "Typed NavigationStack path; pushes only, never used for sheet-presented focused tasks."
    },
    {
      "name": "Category",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "enum Category: String, Codable, CaseIterable, Sendable { case watch, vinyl, card, wine, sneaker, other }",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Fixed six-case enum, shared schema, free-text subtype carries specificity."
    },
    {
      "name": "effectiveState",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "func effectiveState(for item: Item, subscriptionActive: Bool, activeItemIDs: Set<UUID>) -> ItemDisplayState",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "Pure function, precedence: deleted > locked > incomplete > normal. Must be exhaustively unit tested including tied creationSequence values."
    },
    {
      "name": "activeLockedPartition",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "func activeLockedPartition(items: [Item], freeCapActive: Bool, capSize: Int) -> (active: [Item], locked: [Item])",
      "owning_lane": "data_domain",
      "notes": "O(n) single-pass: filter non-deleted, sort by creationSequence, split at capSize when freeCapActive. Never O(n^2) per-item recomputation."
    },
    {
      "name": "EntitlementState",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "enum EntitlementState: Sendable, Equatable, Codable { case free; case subscribed(expiresAt: Date?); case unknownOffline(lastVerified: CachedEntitlement?) }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "Offline continuity must never silently collapse to free by omission; unknownOffline carries the flat cached snapshot, not another EntitlementState."
    },
    {
      "name": "PhotoStore",
      "kind": "protocol",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "protocol PhotoStore: Sendable { func persistOriginalAndThumbnail(imageData: Data, itemID: UUID) async throws -> PhotoAssetRef; func persistEntryPhoto(imageData: Data, itemID: UUID, entryID: UUID) async throws -> PhotoAssetRef; func removeAssets(forItemID itemID: UUID) async throws; func removeAssets(forEntryID entryID: UUID, itemID: UUID) async throws; func loadImage(_ ref: PhotoAssetRef, preview: Bool) async throws -> Data }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "All paths relative to App Group container root; thumbnails generated off-main via byPreparingThumbnail. Returns PhotoAssetRef, never a bare path string."
    },
    {
      "name": "PDFExportService",
      "kind": "protocol",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "protocol PDFExportService: Sendable { func renderSingleItem(_ itemID: UUID) async throws -> URL; func renderCollection() async throws -> URL; func rasterizedPreviewPages(for fileURL: URL) async throws -> [CGImage] }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "UIGraphicsPDFRenderer off-main for the real file; rasterizedPreviewPages feeds the native SwiftUI paged preview, never embed PDFView."
    },
    {
      "name": "CaptureState",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "enum CaptureState: Sendable { case capturing; case draftCreated(itemID: UUID); case mediaProcessing(itemID: UUID); case awaitingDetails(itemID: UUID, coverPhoto: PhotoAssetRef, suggestion: ClassificationSuggestion?); case saveReady(itemID: UUID); case incomplete(itemID: UUID, reason: String) }",
      "owning_lane": "primary_ui",
      "notes": "Single canonical name/shape. Owned exclusively by CaptureViewModel; Vision result only persists to Item.category if still selected at Save."
    },
    {
      "name": "CaptureViewModel",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class CaptureViewModel { private(set) var state: CaptureState; func photoCaptured(data: Data) async; func selectCategory(_ category: Category); func save(title: String, subtype: String) async; func resumeDraft(itemID: UUID) async }",
      "owning_lane": "primary_ui",
      "notes": "Sole owner of capture state machine and draft mutation; nothing else writes to a draft Item."
    },
    {
      "name": "CatalogViewModel",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class CatalogViewModel { private(set) var activeItems: [Item]; private(set) var lockedCount: Int; private(set) var loadState: LoadState; func load() async; func softDelete(_ itemID: UUID) async; func undoDelete(_ itemID: UUID) async }",
      "owning_lane": "primary_ui",
      "notes": "Renders ItemDisplayState per item via computeDisplayState; never its own ad hoc locked/incomplete checks."
    },
    {
      "name": "LoadState",
      "kind": "enum",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "enum LoadState: Equatable, Sendable { case loading; case loaded; case empty; case error(String) }",
      "owning_lane": "primary_ui",
      "notes": "Shared four-state enum reused across Catalog, Export Preview, Preserved list, Recently Deleted."
    },
    {
      "name": "ItemDetailViewModel",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class ItemDetailViewModel { private(set) var item: Item; private(set) var displayState: ItemDisplayState; func addConditionEntry(note: String, provenanceNote: String?, photoData: Data?, conditionChange: ConditionGrade?) async; func addValueEntry(amount: Decimal, type: ValueType, sourceNote: String?, date: Date, photoData: Data?) async; func savePurchaseFacts(_ facts: PurchaseFacts?) async }",
      "owning_lane": "primary_ui",
      "notes": "Owns three-module state; Value module renders LockedTeaserCard vs live ledger vs read-only-preserved based on displayState + subscription."
    },
    {
      "name": "ExportPreviewViewModel",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class ExportPreviewViewModel { private(set) var pages: [ExportPagePreview]; private(set) var loadState: LoadState; func generate(mode: ExportMode) async; func share() -> URL? }",
      "owning_lane": "primary_ui",
      "notes": "Backed by ExportService.generatePreviewPages; TabView(.page) page-position accessibility sourced directly from ExportPagePreview fields, never PDFView."
    },
    {
      "name": "PaywallViewModel",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "@Observable @MainActor final class PaywallViewModel { private(set) var products: [Product]; private(set) var loadState: LoadState; func purchase(_ product: Product) async; func restore() async }",
      "owning_lane": "primary_ui",
      "notes": "Reads/writes only through EntitlementProvider; never touches ItemRepository directly."
    },
    {
      "name": "PrivacyOverlay",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct PrivacyOverlayModifier: ViewModifier { func body(content: Content) -> some View }",
      "owning_lane": "polish_resilience",
      "notes": "Root-level modifier applied once in App/, driven by scenePhase; covers app-switcher snapshot, not per-screen."
    },
    {
      "name": "CollectionSummaryProvider",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "actor CollectionSummaryProvider { func current() async -> CollectionSummary; func invalidate() async }",
      "owning_lane": "polish_resilience",
      "notes": "Debounced recompute on repository write events; sole source for widget and aggregate displays."
    },
    {
      "name": "WidgetReloadCoordinator",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "actor WidgetReloadCoordinator { func scheduleReload() async }",
      "owning_lane": "polish_resilience",
      "notes": "Coalesces WidgetKit reloads to respect background budget."
    },
    {
      "name": "RecoveryPurgeScheduler",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct RecoveryPurgeScheduler { func purgeEligibleItems(repository: any ItemRepository, now: Date) async throws }",
      "owning_lane": "polish_resilience",
      "notes": "Runs at launch/foreground only; no background task scheduler (no BGTaskScheduler)."
    },
    {
      "name": "ExportDocumentModel",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct ExportDocumentModel: Sendable { let sections: [ExportSection]; let generatedAt: Date }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "The export-formatting boundary: domain models + AppSettings map into this exactly once; renderer and in-app preview both consume it so they can never disagree on dates/values."
    },
    {
      "name": "ExportSection",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct ExportSection: Sendable { let title: String; let formattedEntries: [String]; let photoRefs: [PhotoAssetRef] }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "Plain rendering unit consumed by ExportService; never built ad hoc inline inside the renderer."
    },
    {
      "name": "PrivacyOverlayModifier",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct PrivacyOverlayModifier: ViewModifier { func body(content: Content) -> some View }",
      "owning_lane": "polish_resilience",
      "notes": "Root-level privacy shield for app-switcher snapshots and background transitions."
    },
    {
      "name": "CachedEntitlement",
      "kind": "struct",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "struct CachedEntitlement: Codable, Equatable, Sendable { let isSubscribed: Bool; let expiresAt: Date?; let verifiedAt: Date }",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "RESOLVED this round: flat snapshot replaces a rejected recursive EntitlementState.unknownOffline(lastKnown: EntitlementState?) design. Codex's fix, adopted since nobody defended the recursive shape."
    },
    {
      "name": "isSubscriptionActive",
      "kind": "function",
      "language": "swift",
      "signature": "func isSubscriptionActive(_ state: EntitlementState) -> Bool",
      "owning_lane": "services_utilities",
      "notes": "The ONLY legal conversion from EntitlementState to the plain Bool that computeDisplayState/partitionActiveAndLocked/collectionSummary expect. No lane may inline its own switch over EntitlementState."
    }
  ]
}
```
